'''Jack William Walker''' (born 8 August 1999) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level.

==Background==
Walker was born in [[Leeds]], West Yorkshire, England.

==Club career==
Walker made his début for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[Challenge Cup]] against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] where he scored a hat trick. He made his first Super League début against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]. He then went on to become Leeds' first choice fullback and played in the Rhinos' [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Castleford Tigers]] 24-6 at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.express.co.uk/sport/othersport/863581/Super-League-Grand-Final-match-report-Castleford-Tigers-Leeds-Rhinos |title=Rhinos stun Tigers to win eighth Super League Grand Final |publisher=express.co.uk |date=8 October 2017 |accessdate=8 October 2017}}</ref><ref name="Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=7 October 2017|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=9 October 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=Guardian|accessdate=9 October 2017}}</ref>

===Hull FC===
On 21 September 2023 it was announced that he had signed for [[Hull F.C.]] ahead of the 2024 season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=21 Sep 2023 |title=Hull FC confirm re-signing of Jack Walker as full-back makes cross-city switch |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-fc-confirm-re-signing-of-jack-walker-as-full-back-makes-cross-city-switch |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

===Sheffield Eagles===
On 17 Jan 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Hull Daily Mail|date=17 Jan 2025|author=Daniel Tomlinson|title=Jack Walker signs for Championship club as post-Hull FC move completed|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jack-walker-signs-championship-club-9866434}}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 28 August 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=28 Aug 2025|accessdate=30 Aug 2025|title=Salford sign full-back Walker and prop Shaw|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cgqnd4x89p5o}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 7 January 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=7 Jan 2026|author=Aaron Bower|title=Jack Walker secures new deal for 2026 after signing with Championship big guns|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jack-walker-secures-new-deal-for-2026-after-signing-with-championship-big-guns}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants (Reserves) - loan===
On 14 March 2026 he made a surprise appearance for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield Giants Reserves]] in their match against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington Wolves Reserves]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=15 Mar 2026|author=Aaron Bower|title=Will Jack Walker sign for Huddersfield after surprise reserves appearance?|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jack-walker-huddersfield-giants-transfer}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2018, Walker was selected to play for [[England Knights]] on their tour of Papua New Guinea. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Oil Search National Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref>
